datasets.name: prod_bottom10_margin
queryLines:
SELECT
  ProductID,
  ProductName,
  ProductCategory,
  ListPrice,
  BaseCost,
  UnitMargin,
  ActualGrossMarginPct
FROM
  Pricing_analysis.data.product_profitability
ORDER BY
  ActualGrossMarginPct ASC
LIMIT 10

datasets.name: product_profitability
queryLines:
SELECT
  ProductID,
  ProductName,
  ProductCategory,
  ListPrice,
  BaseCost,
  UnitMargin,
  MarginPct,
  MarkupPct,
  TotalOrders,
  TotalUnitsSold,
  TotalRevenue,
  TotalGrossProfit,
  ActualGrossMarginPct
FROM
  Pricing_analysis.data.product_profitability
ORDER BY
  TotalGrossProfit DESC

datasets.name: orders_sql
queryLines:
SELECT
  OrderMonthDate,
  OrderYear,
  OrderMonth,
  ProductCategory,
  Region,
  ProductName,
  SUM(Revenue) AS TotalRevenue,
  SUM(GrossProfit) AS TotalGrossProfit,
  SUM(COGS) AS TotalCOGS,
  COUNT(*) AS TotalOrders,
  AVG(GrossMarginPct) AS AvgGrossMarginPct,
  AVG(AvgSellingPrice) AS AvgSellingPrice
FROM
  Pricing_analysis.data.orders_master
GROUP BY
  OrderMonthDate,
  OrderYear,
  OrderMonth,
  ProductCategory,
  Region,
  ProductName

lines: Three years of order history · $871K total revenue · 54.5% gross margin · 200 customers · 50 products across 5 categories

frame.title: Revenue by Year
frame.description: Year-over-year total revenue — track growth or decline across 2023, 2024, and 2025.

frame.title: Revenue by Category
frame.description: Which categories drive the most volume? Consumables, Subscriptions, Merchandise, Grinders & Brewers, and Accessories.

frame.title: Monthly Revenue & Gross Profit Trend
frame.description: Track the gap between revenue and gross profit month by month. A widening gap signals rising COGS relative to pricing.

lines: How has revenue evolved across 2023, 2024, and 2025? Explore year-over-year trends, seasonal cycles, and whether each passing year has grown our top-line and maintained our margin health.

frame.title: Monthly Revenue by Year (YoY Comparison)
frame.description: Each line represents one fiscal year. Overlapping months reveal seasonal peaks and allow direct year-to-year comparison.

frame.title: Total Revenue by Year
frame.description: Is the business growing? 2025 is a partial year — factor in the mid-year cutoff when comparing against full years.

frame.title: Avg Gross Margin % by Year
frame.description: Is our pricing discipline holding? A stable or rising margin year-on-year means we are not sacrificing profitability for volume.

frame.title: Monthly Avg Markup % Trend
frame.description: Markup % shows how far above base cost we are pricing. Dips signal pricing pressure; spikes may indicate mix shift toward premium products.

lines: Not all products are created equal. Explore which categories and products deliver the highest margins, which are underpriced relative to their cost, and where pricing power is strongest across all 50 SKUs and 5 categories.

frame.title: Revenue by Category
frame.description: Which categories drive top-line volume? Revenue from highest to lowest category.

frame.title: Avg Gross Margin % by Category
frame.description: Which categories protect profitability best? Higher margin = more pricing power per dollar of cost.

frame.title: Avg Markup % by Category
frame.description: Markup % is the pricing power signal: how far above base cost are we charging? Categories with high markup have strong pricing leverage.

frame.title: Avg Selling Price by Category
frame.description: The actual price customers paid per unit on average. Compare this to list price to detect discounting patterns.

frame.title: Gross Margin % by Category Over Time
frame.description: Has each category’s margin improved, declined, or held steady? Diverging lines may reveal pricing strategy inconsistencies across the catalog.

lines: Our business serves customers in East and West regions. Are they buying the same products at the same prices? Do regional pricing strategies differ — and should they? This section answers those questions with data.

frame.title: Revenue by Region
frame.description: How does East vs. West split in total revenue? A large imbalance may point to market concentration risk.

frame.title: Avg Gross Margin % by Region
frame.description: Do East and West customers generate the same margin? Regional differences in margin often reflect different product mix or discount practices.

frame.title: Monthly Revenue by Region
frame.description: Track both regions on the same timeline. Are they growing in parallel, or is one pulling ahead? Divergence after a certain date may signal a regional pricing change.

frame.title: Total Orders by Region
frame.description: Volume of transactions per region. Combine with revenue to compute average order value — are East customers buying more per order than West?

frame.title: Avg Markup % by Region
frame.description: Pricing power signal per region. A lower markup in one region may indicate stronger competition, a different customer segment, or an opportunity to reprice.
